2604.04999.title: PRIME: Prototype-Driven Multimodal Pretraining for Cancer Prognosis with Missing Modalities
2604.04999.paper_summary: Multimodal self-supervised pretraining offers a promising route to cancer prognosis by integrating histopathology whole-slide images, gene expression, and pathology reports, yet most existing approaches require fully paired and complete inputs. In practice, clinical cohorts are fragmented and often miss one or more modalities, limiting both supervised fusion and scalable multimodal pretraining. We propose PRIME, a missing-aware multimodal self-supervised pretraining framework that learns robust and transferable representations from partially observed cohorts. PRIME maps heterogeneous modality embeddings into a unified token space and introduces a shared prototype memory bank for latent-space semantic imputation via patient-level consensus retrieval, producing structurally aligned tokens without reconstructing raw signals. Two complementary pretraining objectives: inter-modality alignment and post-fusion consistency under structured missingness augmentation, jointly learn representations that remain predictive under arbitrary modality subsets. We evaluate PRIME on The Cancer Genome Atlas with label-free pretraining on 32 cancer types and downstream 5-fold evaluation on five cohorts across overall survival prediction, 3-year mortality classification, and 3-year recurrence classification. PRIME achieves the best macro-average performance among all compared methods, reaching 0.653 C-index, 0.689 AUROC, and 0.637 AUROC on the three tasks, respectively, while improving robustness under test-time missingness and supporting parameter-efficient and label-efficient adaptation. These results support missing-aware multimodal pretraining as a practical strategy for prognosis modeling in fragmented clinical data settings.
2604.04999.paper_summary_zh: 多模態自我監督預訓練為癌症預後提供了一條有前景的路徑，通過整合組織病理學全幻燈片圖像、基因表達和病理報告，但大多數現有方法需要完全配對和完整的輸入。<lb/>在實踐中，臨床隊列是片段化的，並且經常缺少一種或多種模態，這限制了監督融合和可擴展的多模態預訓練。<lb/>我們提出了PRIME，一個缺失感知的多模態自我監督預訓練框架，從部分觀察的隊列中學習穩健且可轉移的表示。<lb/>PRIME將異質模態嵌入映射到統一的標記空間，並通過患者級共識檢索引入共享原型記憶庫，用於潛在空間語義插補，生成結構對齊的標記，而無需重建原始信號。<lb/>兩個互補的預訓練目標：模態間對齊和結構缺失增強下的後融合一致性，聯合學習在任意模態子集下仍然具有預測性的表示。<lb/>我們在癌症基因組圖譜上評估PRIME，進行了32種癌症類型的無標籤預訓練，並在五個隊列上進行了下游的5折評估，涵蓋整體生存預測、3年死亡率分類和3年復發分類。<lb/>PRIME在所有比較方法中達到了最佳的宏平均性能，三個任務的C指數達到0.653，AUROC達到0.689，以及AUROC達到0.637，同時在測試時缺失情況下提高了穩健性，並支持參數高效和標籤高效的適應。<lb/>這些結果支持缺失感知的多模態預訓練作為在片段化臨床數據環境中進行預後建模的實用策略。
2604.04999.author: Kai Yu et.al.
2604.04999.authors: Kai Yu, Shuang Zhou, Yiran Song, Zaifu Zhan, Jie Peng, Kaixiong Zhou, Tianlong Chen, Feng Xie, Meng Wang, Huazhu Fu, Mingquan Lin, Rui Zhang
